commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Gold. God is the head of Christ, (1 Corinthians xi. 3.) and is most pure. (N.)
The guards of Solomon were powdered with gold dust. (Josephus, Jewish Antiquities)
Branches. Elatæ, or fruits of the male palm-tree. (Theodoret) (Pliny, xiii. 4.)

commentaries.author: Julian of Eclanum
commentaries.content: Hair is also represented by another comparison, wherein the shape of its locks seems to mimic the brilliance of gold. “His hair is like waves of palms,” so that they would surely seem to be curly and golden. - "Commentary on the Song of Songs, Fragment 10"

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
